commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: 22. "He made their rains hail" (ver. 32). It is a similar expression to the former, "He made their land frogs;" except that the whole land was not actually turned into frogs, though the whole of the rain may have been turned into hail. "A burning fire in their land:" understand, "He sent."

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Rain. Which fall in Egypt, though less frequently. (Calmet) (Wisdom xvi. 17.)
